evals.prompt: I just set up foundryd and registered a project called 'billing-api'. I want to check if its quality gates pass without making any changes. How do I do that?
evals.expected_output: Should recommend `foundry validate billing-api` and explain the output format (per-gate pass/fail, exit code 1 on failure). Should NOT suggest iterate or run.

evals.prompt: My last foundry run finished overnight. I want to see what happened — which projects succeeded, which failed, and why. Where do I look?
evals.expected_output: Should recommend `foundry history` first, then `foundry trace <event_id> --verbose` for drilling into specifics. May mention the audit report at ~/.foundry/audits/runs/YYYY-MM-DD/summary.md as a fallback for more detail.

evals.prompt: I want foundry to do an AI-assisted quality improvement on my 'checkout-service' project but I'm nervous about it pushing changes. Can I preview what it would do first?
evals.expected_output: Should explain two options: (1) `foundry run --project checkout-service --throttle dry_run` to simulate the full chain with no side effects, or (2) disable push in the registry with `foundry registry edit checkout-service --push false` so iterate runs but doesn't push. Should NOT suggest `foundry emit` since convenience commands are preferred.

evals.prompt: I need to release my 'foundry' project. The changelog has a mix of bug fixes and a new feature. How does foundry handle that?
evals.expected_output: Should recommend `foundry release foundry` and explain that without --bump, the agent reads the changelog and determines the appropriate bump automatically. May mention `--bump minor` if the user wants to override. Should note the release action must be enabled in the registry.

evals.prompt: CI is failing on my 'web-app' project after a recent merge. Can foundry help fix it?
evals.expected_output: Should recommend `foundry pipeline web-app` and explain it checks GitHub Actions status, fetches failure logs, and invokes Claude to diagnose and fix CI failures automatically.
